localizations.title: The Ascent
localizations.hiddenObject_4: A number is engraved upon the base of the Pilgrim Statue.
localizations.hint_1: Add a single milestone to your current position.
localizations.hint_9: You have an item in your backpack that can keep these wolves at bay.
localizations.hint_15: Just like before, you need to leap over the crevasse.
localizations.hint_111_first: Use an item from your backpack to help you climb using the correct grips.
localizations.hint_111: The visible grips match the shape of the engravings on the base of the Pilgrim Statue (21).
localizations.hint_4: Use an item from your backpack to help you avoid sinking in deep snow.
localizations.hint_2: Follow your grandfather's advice and jump over the next crevasse.
localizations.hint_92: Use an item with your name to mark your accomplishment.
localizations.hint_81: Use an item from your backpack to help you navigate when you can't see very far in front of you.
localizations.hint_31: Use an item from your backpack to keep them busy so you can escape.
localizations.hint_60_first: You can retrace your steps by flipping the cards over.
localizations.hint_60: The orientation of the tracks will assist you in arranging them.
localizations.hint_48_first: Use an item from your backpack to get a better view of the stones on the mountain visible in the background. These stones form a number.
localizations.hint_48: The mountain in the background is one of the Twin Mountains peaks. You also need to figure out the shape of the other mountain.
localizations.hint_45: One card from the deck refers to the north.
localizations.hint_14: Use an item from your backpack to identify signifcant features along your route.
localizations.hint_17_first: Avoid the cracks and the areas next to them.
localizations.hint_17: By staying as far away from the cracks as possible, your route forms a number.
localizations.hint_99: Use the memories of your grandfather to select the correct pennants.
localizations.hint_34_first: Use the memorial to map out the routes that the previous climbers took before reaching their ultimate destinations. These routes start from the west and the south.
localizations.hint_34: The number next to each name indicates the number of locations the climbers passed through in order to avoid the crevasses.
localizations.hint_machine_compass_first: Use your notes to guide you.
localizations.hint_machine_compass: You start your journey in the east and then head west.
localizations.answer_hint_1: To progress from one milestone to the next, add 1 to the value of this card. 1 + 1 = 2
localizations.answer_hint_9: To keep the wolves at bay, use the flare from your backpack (+22). 9 + 22 = 31
localizations.answer_hint_15: To progress while jumping over the crevasse, add 2 to the value of this card. 15 + 2 = 17
localizations.answer_hint_111: Use the ice axe from your backpack (+15) to follow the route marked on the base of the Pilgrim Statue (21). 15 + 10 + 5 + 7 + 7 + 4 = 48
localizations.answer_hint_4: Use the snowshoes from your backpack (+10) to trudge atop the deep snow. 10 + 4 = 14.
localizations.answer_hint_2: The memory of a previous hike (40) indicates that you must jump over the crevasse. To do this, add 2 to the value of that card instead of 1. 2 + 2 = 4
localizations.answer_hint_92: Plant your flag (7) on the peak. 92 + 7 = 99
localizations.answer_hint_81: To navigate through the snowstorm, press the Backpack button and then the compass.
localizations.answer_hint_31: Use some food to distract the wolves and make your escape. You have canned food in your backpack (+29). 31 + 29 = 60.
localizations.answer_hint_60: To see the tracks you've left behind, flip over cards 9, 31, and 60. Then arrange them so the tracks always face the same direction. Their shape forms the number 56.
localizations.answer_hint_48: Press the Backpack button and select the binoculars to get a better view of the mountain, enabling you to make out a number. Since this mountain is one of the Twin Mountains, as shown on the map, it means the second mountain is identical to the first. The Twin Mountains, therefore, form a number. Take the corresponding card.
localizations.answer_hint_45: North is indicated by the letter N, as shown on the compass that you can see when pressing the Backpack button.
localizations.answer_hint_14: To view your route more clearly, press the Backpack button, select the binoculars, and scan card 14 with your device.
localizations.answer_hint_17: Advance up to the flags while while staying 2 spaces away from the cracks. Your route forms the number 45.
localizations.answer_hint_99: Based on the memories you have of your grandfather's outfits (93, 40, and D), select the blue, yellow, and red pennants.
localizations.answer_hint_34: Map out the routes taken by the four climbers depicted on the memorial (43). The first climber started in the west, and visited Thrush Needles and Mountledge Peak. The second climber started in the south, and visited Rover and St. Bernard Pass. The third climber started in the south and visited Rover, Peak of the Just, and Hope's Edge. The fourth climber started in the south and visited Guy Burrow, Thrush Needles, and Mugwort Peak.
localizations.answer_hint_machine_compass: Avoiding the routes taken by the previous climbers, you blaze a new path. Begin in the east, then move west, north, west again, southwest, and southeast.
localizations.machine_34: You have mapped out the routes taken by the four climbers.
localizations.machine_bag_open: You retrieve the map from your backpack.
localizations.machine_compass: You push through the snowstorm and lift your head high.
localizations.END: You have conquered Mount Celestine! Your initials are proudly displayed at the summit, alongside the colors used to honor your grandfather. Alice Delaroche becomes the first person to reach the peak in the winter, and the trail leading there is now known as the Theodore Delaroche Memorial Trail.
localizations.machine_34_open: You copy the routes taken by previous climbers into your notebook in order to help you reach the peak of Mount Celestine.
localizations.machine_34_selectest: Begin by tracing the routes taken by the previous climbers.
localizations.machine_compass_error: You get lost in the snowstorm and find yourself back where you began.
localizations.machine_jumelles_stop: This snowstorm makes it hard to see where you're going.
